exercises.name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart and about two feet from the wall.
Slide down until your knees are bent to roughly 90 degrees, as if sitting in an invisible chair.
Hold, keeping knees tracking over ankles and lower back flat against the wall.
safetyNotes: Stop if sharp anterior knee pain occurs; discomfort in the quads is expected, joint pain is not.

exercises.name: Spanish Squat
instructions: Loop a heavy resistance band around a sturdy anchor at knee height and around the back of both knees.
Step back so the band is taut, then sit into a squat while leaning back slightly against the band's pull, keeping shins as vertical as possible.
Hold the bottom position, feeling the tension concentrate on the quads just above the knee.
safetyNotes: Commonly used for patellar tendon rehab; keep shins vertical to bias the quads over the knee.

exercises.name: Eccentric Step-Down
instructions: Stand on a low step or box on one leg, other foot hovering just off the edge.
Slowly lower the free foot to lightly tap the floor over 3-5 seconds, keeping the standing knee tracking over the toes.
Push back up to standing on the box and repeat, then switch legs.
safetyNotes: Use a low step (4-6in) until control is consistent; don't let the knee cave inward.

exercises.name: Banded Terminal Knee Extension
instructions: Anchor a light band at knee height behind you and loop it around the back of one knee, standing with a soft bend in that knee.
Straighten the knee fully against the band's pull, squeezing the quad at the top.
Return with control and repeat, then switch legs.
safetyNotes: Low-load and joint-friendly; a good entry point after a knee flare-up.

exercises.name: Slow-Tempo Box Squat
instructions: Stand in front of a box or bench with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower for a slow 4-5 second count until you lightly touch the box, keeping the chest up.
Stand back up at a normal pace and repeat.
safetyNotes: Use a box height that allows full control; don't let the knees drift past the toes excessively.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Sit the hips back and down, keeping chest up, until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through the whole foot to stand back up.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold a dumbbell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Squat down between your knees, keeping the torso upright and elbows brushing the inside of the knees at the bottom.
Drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Set a barbell across the upper back and step out of the rack, feet shoulder-width apart.
Sit the hips back and down until thighs are at least parallel to the floor, keeping the chest up.
Drive through the floor to return to standing.
safetyNotes: Use a rack with safety pins; do not round the lower back under load.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand a couple feet in front of a bench and rest the top of one foot on it behind you.
Lower straight down until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front foot to stand back up, then switch legs.
safetyNotes: Keep the front knee tracking over the foot; expect more balance demand than a standard lunge.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
instructions: Step forward into a lunge, lowering the back knee toward the floor.
Drive through the front foot to bring the back leg through into the next lunge step.
Continue alternating legs while moving forward.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold a barbell at hip height with a shoulder-width grip.
Push the hips back while lowering the bar along the shins, keeping the back flat and knees softly bent.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward to stand back up.
safetyNotes: Do not round the lower back; stop if sharp back or knee pain occurs.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand, standing on the opposite leg with a soft bend in the knee.
Hinge forward, extending the free leg back for counterbalance, until torso and leg are roughly parallel to the floor.
Reverse the motion by driving the hips forward to return to standing, then switch sides.
safetyNotes: Keep hips square to the floor; use a lighter load than the bilateral version.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit with upper back against a bench, a barbell (or bodyweight) across the hips.
Drive through the heels to lift the hips until torso is parallel to the floor, squeezing the glutes at the top.
Lower with control and repeat.
safetyNotes: Pad the bar across the hips; avoid overextending the lower back at the top.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, kettlebell on the floor slightly in front of you.
Hinge to grip the bell, then hike it back between your legs before snapping the hips forward explosively to swing it to chest height.
Let the bell float back down and hinge to absorb the next rep.
safetyNotes: Power comes from hip snap, not the arms or lower back; keep the spine neutral throughout.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
instructions: Kneel with ankles anchored (a partner, bar, or strap holding your heels down).
Keeping hips extended and body in a straight line, lower your torso toward the floor as slowly as possible by resisting with the hamstrings.
Use your hands to catch yourself at the bottom and push back to the start.
safetyNotes: Very high eccentric hamstring demand; start with only 2-3 reps and expect significant delayed soreness at first.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Stance Hold
instructions: Stand tall and shift weight onto one leg, lifting the other foot slightly off the ground.
Keep a soft bend in the standing knee and brace the core to stay steady.
Hold for the prescribed duration, then switch legs.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Y-Balance Reach
instructions: Stand on one leg with a soft knee bend.
Reach the free leg forward, then to the side, then behind you, tapping the floor lightly each time without losing balance.
Return to center between reaches, then switch legs.

exercises.name: Wobble Board Single-Leg Balance
instructions: Stand on a wobble board or balance disc with one foot, other foot lifted.
Make small continuous corrections to keep the board's edge off the floor.
Hold for the prescribed duration, then switch legs.
safetyNotes: Stand near a wall or sturdy object to catch yourself the first few sessions.

exercises.name: Warrior III Balance Pose
instructions: From standing, hinge forward at the hips while lifting one leg straight behind you.
Reach arms forward until torso and back leg form a straight line parallel to the floor.
Hold, keeping hips level, then return to standing and switch legs.

exercises.name: Tandem (Heel-to-Toe) Walk
instructions: Walk forward in a straight line, placing the heel of one foot directly in front of the toes of the other with each step.
Keep your gaze forward and arms out to the sides for balance.
Continue for the prescribed distance or time, then turn and repeat.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Switch
instructions: Sit on the floor with front leg bent 90 degrees in front of you and back leg bent 90 degrees behind you.
Keeping both sit bones down, rotate the legs to switch which leg is in front.
Repeat the switch for the prescribed reps, moving with control.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions: Step into a long lunge, dropping the back knee to the floor.
Plant both hands inside the front foot, then rotate one arm up toward the ceiling, following it with your eyes.
Return the hand to the floor, straighten the front leg to stretch the hamstring, then step forward and switch sides.

exercises.name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Rock (Knee-to-Wall)
instructions: Kneel in a half-kneeling position with the front foot flat on the floor a few inches from a wall.
Keeping the front heel down, rock the knee forward toward (or past) the wall.
Move through a controlled range for the prescribed reps, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Thoracic Rotation (Open Book)
instructions: Lie on your side with knees stacked and bent 90 degrees, arms extended together in front of you.
Keeping knees stacked, open the top arm across your body toward the floor behind you, following it with your eyes.
Return to start and repeat, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Kneeling Hip Flexor Stretch
instructions: Kneel on one knee with the other foot planted in front, both at 90 degrees.
Shift your weight forward until you feel a stretch through the front of the hip and thigh of the kneeling leg.
Hold, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Zone 2 Cycling
instructions: Ride at a steady pace where you could hold a full conversation without gasping (roughly 60-70% max heart rate).
Maintain that effort continuously for the prescribed duration.
Cool down with a few minutes of easy pedaling.

exercises.name: Zone 2 Trail Hiking
instructions: Hike at a steady, conversational pace on varied terrain.
Take smaller controlled steps on downhill sections to manage impact.
Maintain that effort continuously for the prescribed duration.

exercises.name: Steady-State Rollerblading
instructions: Skate at a comfortable, sustainable pace, keeping knees soft and pushing off through the full stride.
Maintain that effort continuously for the prescribed duration.
Wear protective gear and choose a smooth, low-traffic path.
safetyNotes: Requires good balance and braking skill; wear wrist, knee, and helmet protection.

exercises.name: Steady-State Jogging
instructions: Jog at a steady, conversational pace on a flat surface or treadmill.
Maintain an upright posture and relaxed shoulders.
Maintain that effort continuously for the prescribed duration.
safetyNotes: Higher-impact than cycling or rollerblading; scale back if knees or shins are cranky.

exercises.name: Stair Climbing
instructions: Climb at a steady, sustainable cadence on a stair machine or a real stairwell.
Keep your torso upright rather than leaning on the handrails.
Maintain that effort continuously for the prescribed duration.

exercises.name: Air Squat Endurance Set
instructions: Perform bodyweight squats continuously for the prescribed time cap, aiming for as many quality reps as possible.
Keep full range of motion and consistent tempo even as fatigue builds.
Rest only as needed to keep form from breaking down.
safetyNotes: Stop the set early if form degrades rather than grinding out sloppy reps.

exercises.name: Continuous Step-Up Circuit
instructions: Step up onto a box or bench with one leg, driving through the heel to stand tall on top.
Step back down with control and immediately repeat, alternating legs continuously.
Maintain a steady rhythm for the prescribed duration.

exercises.name: Alternating Lateral Bounds (Skater Hops)
instructions: Push off one leg, bounding sideways and landing softly on the opposite leg, letting the free leg trail behind for balance.
Immediately bound back the other direction without pausing.
Continue at a sustainable rhythm for the prescribed duration.
safetyNotes: Land softly with a bent knee each time; reduce range if landings feel unstable.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders.
Drive one knee toward the chest, then quickly switch legs in a running-like motion.
Keep the hips low and core braced for the prescribed duration.

exercises.name: Rope Jumping
instructions: Turn the rope with the wrists, jumping just high enough to clear it on each pass.
Keep a light, springy rhythm and soft knees.
Continue for the prescribed duration, resting briefly if rhythm breaks down.

exercises.name: Agility Ladder Icky Shuffle
instructions: Lay an agility ladder flat on the ground.
Move through each rung with quick in-in-out foot patterns, staying on the balls of the feet.
Focus on speed and precision rather than height; repeat down the full ladder.

exercises.name: Reaction Ball Drop-Catch
instructions: Hold a six-sided reaction ball at chest height and drop it in front of you.
React as it bounces unpredictably, moving your feet to get in position and catch it before the second bounce.
Reset and repeat for the prescribed reps.

exercises.name: Wall Toss Reaction Catch
instructions: Stand a few feet from a wall and throw a ball (a reaction ball adds unpredictability) against it at varying angles.
React to the rebound and catch it, moving your feet as needed.
Repeat continuously for the prescribed duration, solo, no partner required.

exercises.name: Color/Light-Cue Sprint Start
instructions: Set a phone app, reaction light, or timer to display a random color or cue after an unpredictable delay.
Start in an athletic ready stance and sprint a short distance the instant the cue appears.
Walk back and repeat for the prescribed reps.
safetyNotes: Warm up sprint mechanics with a few submaximal efforts before going full speed.

exercises.name: Random Cone Touch Drill
instructions: Arrange 4-5 cones in a semicircle a few steps out from a center point.
Have a partner call, or a phone app randomly select, a cone to touch; sprint out, touch it, and return to center.
Reset and react to the next call, staying light on your feet between reps.

exercises.name: Push-Up
instructions: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Lower your chest toward the floor, keeping the body in a straight line.
Push back up to the starting position.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms facing away.
Pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar, leading with the chest.
Lower back down with control to a full hang.
safetyNotes: Use a resistance band for assistance if unable to complete a full rep with good form.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and hand on a bench, holding a dumbbell in the other hand with the arm extended.
Pull the dumbbell up toward the hip, squeezing the shoulder blade at the top.
Lower with control and repeat, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Overhead Press
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at shoulder height, core braced.
Press the weights straight overhead until arms are fully extended.
Lower back to shoulder height with control and repeat.
safetyNotes: Avoid over-arching the lower back; brace the core throughout.

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry
instructions: Pick up a heavy dumbbell or kettlebell in each hand, standing tall with shoulders back.
Walk forward for the prescribed distance or time, keeping the core braced and steps controlled.
Set the weights down with control at the end.

exercises.name: Box Step-Off Soft Landing
instructions: Stand on a low box or step and step off, letting one or both feet land on the floor.
Absorb the landing by bending the hips, knees, and ankles, aiming for a silent, controlled touchdown.
Reset and repeat, gradually increasing box height as landings stay quiet and controlled.
safetyNotes: Start with a low box (6-12in); progress height only once landings are consistently soft and controlled.

exercises.name: Broad Jump with Stuck Landing
instructions: Load the hips back and swing the arms back, then jump forward as far as possible.
Land on both feet simultaneously, absorbing the impact by bending hips and knees.
Hold ('stick') the landing for a full second before resetting for the next rep.
safetyNotes: Prioritize a controlled, balanced landing over jump distance; reset fully between reps.

exercises.name: Unstable-Surface Reactive Stance
instructions: Stand on a foam pad, Bosu ball, or other unstable surface, single-leg or narrow split-stance.
Let the surface continuously perturb your balance and react to re-stabilize without stepping off.
Hold for the prescribed duration, then switch legs or stance.
safetyNotes: Stand near a wall or sturdy object to catch yourself; no partner or push needed.

exercises.name: Backward Fall Recovery (Breakfall Practice)
instructions: From a squat, tuck the chin and round the spine as you sit back and roll onto a mat, landing across the back diagonally rather than straight down the spine.
Slap the mat with the arms at a 45-degree angle as you land to dissipate force, keeping the head off the ground throughout.
Roll back up to standing and reset.
safetyNotes: Learn on a padded mat; keep the chin tucked so the head never contacts the ground.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Drop Landing
instructions: Stand on one leg on a small box (4-8in), other foot lifted.
Step off and drop onto the same leg, absorbing the landing with a soft, deep bend through the hip, knee, and ankle.
Stick the landing for a full second before resetting, then switch legs.
safetyNotes: High single-leg landing load; master the double-leg box step-off first and keep box height low.
